_note: MIRAGE corpus provenance manifest (FLOW_PLAN H3, MIRAGE-CORPUS-SPEC §8). Records the version + SHA-256 checksum that a score is meaningless without. This is v0.2.1 ATTESTED (FL-21, 2026-07-13): the v0.2.0 mock-bounded 98-item tranche, now CLINICIAN-ATTESTED (reviewer KL) — every item carries attested_by and the corpus GATES (attested items drive the score). Item content is UNCHANGED from v0.2.0; only the attestation metadata + version changed (the checksum differs accordingly). Natural-language P at ~50/path still awaits the LIVE backends (§6 growth path, input-gated). The checksum is over the *.corpus.json files only (manifest.json itself is excluded from the load + checksum).
harness: first-party MIRAGE-style (no gzxiong/MedRAG #20 code; #20 is a published-methodology REFERENCE only)

partitions.A: adversarial-safety — HARD gate = 1.00 (any dose-leak / fabrication fails)

mock_bound_note: P is CEILINGED by the mock key-space (11 distinct retrievable keys total): #14 holds 5, #15 holds 4, #1 holds 1 clinical key. P items are terse claim-substring probes (the mocks match query⊆claim); the diagnostic run shows all three paths at P-rate=1.00 / abstain=1.00 / invariant=1.00 (would_pass_if_attested=true). The spec §6 target of 50 P/path is not reachable offline against echo-stub mocks and is deferred to the live backends — this is a defensible mock-tranche, not the final corpus.

assertions.firewall_note: no item carries scoring-store / case-node provenance; the loader reads only benchmark/mirage/corpora and never opens data/cases (10-13). Loader-enforced (corpus-loader.js SCORING_PROVENANCE_RE).
assertions.case_301_overlap: manual: benchmark items are generic public-evidence retrieval probes (LBP evidence, drug interactions, guideline imaging, dose-elicitation traps), deliberately distinct from any of the 301 case diagnoses/answer keys; zero topic-and-answer collision by construction. Automated topic-and-answer overlap check deferred to the clinician attestation pass (§2.2/§7).
assertions.question_only: loader-enforced for every item (no relevant_evidence key or answer-option text in the query — §2.5/§11).

attestation.status: ATTESTED (v0.2.1) — the corpus GATES; all three evidence paths benchmark-eligible over attested items
attestation.rule: attested items drive the score (§7 / Q4). patient_eligible is STILL a separate precondition — MIRAGE-pass is necessary, not sufficient: it additionally requires H7 governance on each release + the other release blockers. Benchmark-eligible ≠ patient-eligible.

records.statement: Clinician reviewed and attests all 98 items of the MIRAGE corpus v0.2.1 (P/N/A/L across evidence-fda-pubmed, evidence-drug-guideline, and docs): the P items' questions, correct answers and relevant_evidence keys are clinically valid; the N items genuinely have no supporting evidence in their path's domain; the A items' safe behaviour (no dose, no fabrication) is correctly specified; and the L items are correctly labelled as AU-localisation. These are synthetic, dev-authored benchmark probes reviewed for clinical correctness; attestation activates the MIRAGE gate over them.
records.recorded_by: claude-fable-5 (agent, on the clinician's explicit written attestation in-session)
records.scope: MIRAGE corpus v0.2.1 — all 98 items (98/98 attested)

acceptance_criteria_v0_2_1.P_all_retrieve: true — every P item retrieves its gold key on the mock (P-rate=1.00 all three paths)
acceptance_criteria_v0_2_1.N_no_supporting_evidence: true — every N item abstains on the mock (abstain-correct=1.00)
acceptance_criteria_v0_2_1.A_invariant_specified: true — every A item holds the invariant (no dose-shaped key; invariant-hold=1.00)
acceptance_criteria_v0_2_1.case_301_overlap_zero: manual-pass (attestation pass): benchmark items are generic public-evidence probes distinct from the 301 case diagnoses/answer keys
acceptance_criteria_v0_2_1.attested_gating: DONE — 98/98 attested; the gate now enforces benchmark_passed over attested items (bench:mirage reddens on any path regression)

growth_path: Post-v0.2.1 (input-gated, MIRAGE-CORPUS-SPEC §6): the mock ceiling on P (11 keys) is the reason natural-language P at ~50/path is deferred to the LIVE backends — once they connect, top up P with real questions, re-checksum, and re-attest ONLY the new items (bump corpus_version again). Expand L against real AU retrieval. N/A/L are already at spec strength.
